title: L002: Add chaos test framework and dependency pod kill
summary: Well-structured chaos E2E test framework with 3 pod-kill scenarios (MariaDB, Memcached, OpenBao). All YAML files have correct SPDX headers and CC-0047 references. Chainsaw config correctly separates chaos settings from happy-path. PodChaos CRDs use correct label selectors and namespace targeting (including cross-namespace for OpenBao). JMESPath condition assertions follow the established pattern. Catch blocks provide comprehensive diagnostics. Memcached no-regression test correctly asserts all 6 conditions remain True. Documentation is thorough with accurate diagnostics matrix. Minor deviations from plan (CR name keystone-chaos-mc vs keystone-chaos-cache, timeout values matching happy-path instead of plan) are reasonable and internally consistent.

tests_checklist.item: E2E test definitions exist for all 3 planned scenarios (SC-CHAOS-001, SC-CHAOS-002, SC-CHAOS-003)
Each scenario has unique CR name and database name preventing parallel execution conflicts
Baseline assertion (Ready=True) before chaos injection in all 3 tests
Degradation assertions verify correct sub-condition transitions (DatabaseReady=False, SecretsReady=False)
Recovery assertions verify return to healthy state (sub-condition=True, Ready=True/AllReady)
Memcached no-regression test asserts all 6 conditions remain True during outage
Catch blocks present on all assert steps with diagnostic output
PodChaos CRD explicitly deleted before recovery assertion to ensure fault is lifted

code_quality_checklist.item: SPDX Apache-2.0 headers on all YAML files
CC-0047 feature ID referenced in all test files and config
Step comments follow '# ── Step N: Description ──' format
Fixture files use 00-/01- numeric prefix ordering
apiVersion v1alpha2 for Configuration matches happy-path (with DECISION comment)
apiVersion v1alpha1 for Test kind matches existing pattern
Consistent naming convention across scenarios (db, mc, bao)

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in test fixtures
PodChaos CRDs use label selectors not pod names (no targeting by UID)
Cross-namespace targeting for OpenBao documented with rationale
Catch block scripts use '|| true' to prevent diagnostic failures from masking test results

architecture_checklist.item: Separate chainsaw-config.yaml with chaos-specific settings (higher timeouts, lower parallelism)
Makefile target correctly placed after e2e target
Test directory structure mirrors happy-path convention (tests/e2e-chaos/<scenario>/)
Documentation covers framework, scenarios, patterns, and adding new scenarios

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic across scenarios (each test validates distinct behavior)
No unnecessary abstractions — tests are self-contained YAML definitions
README.md is minor scope addition but follows existing tests/e2e/ convention and adds value

fail_fast_checklist.item: failFast: true in chainsaw config stops on first failure
Baseline assertion (Step 2) catches unhealthy initial state before chaos injection

defensive_checklist.item: Catch blocks collect diagnostics from multiple sources (pods, events, chaos status)
OpenBao catch blocks check pods in openbao-system namespace (correct cross-namespace)
Scripts in catch blocks use 2>&1 || true to prevent diagnostic command failures from cascading

issues_found.description: Makefile e2e-chaos target missing CC-0047 reference comment. Plan task 1.1 explicitly specifies 'with comment referencing CC-0047'. The existing e2e target also lacks a comment (so this is pattern-consistent), but the plan was explicit. The tempest-test target shows the comment pattern.
issues_found.fix: Add comment line above the target: '# e2e-chaos runs Chaos Mesh E2E tests against a deployed cluster (CC-0047).'
issues_found.evidence:
.PHONY: e2e-chaos
e2e-chaos:
	chainsaw test --config tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e-chaos/

issues_found.description: Chainsaw config timeouts for delete, error, and exec are 30s instead of the 60s specified in plan task 1.1. However, these match the happy-path config (tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml) which also uses 30s for all three. The implementer chose pattern consistency over plan compliance — a reasonable decision, but it should be documented.
issues_found.fix: No change needed — matching happy-path is the right call. Optionally add a brief comment noting the deviation: '# delete/error/exec match happy-path (30s); plan specified 60s but no chaos-specific reason to differ.'

issues_found.description: Memcached CR name is keystone-chaos-mc instead of keystone-chaos-cache specified in plan task 3.1 and review criteria. The 'mc' abbreviation is consistent with the db/bao naming pattern and all references (test files, docs, README) are internally consistent. This is a deliberate and sensible deviation.
issues_found.fix: No change needed — keystone-chaos-mc is more consistent with the db/bao naming convention. The plan's keystone-chaos-cache would be the outlier.

issues_found.description: Test file header comments reference only 'REQ-001 (CC-0047)' for all 3 scenarios instead of the scenario-specific requirements from the plan (e.g., SC-CHAOS-001 should reference REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008, REQ-009). The docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md correctly lists per-scenario requirements in the inventory table.
issues_found.fix: Update requirement IDs: mariadb → 'SC-CHAOS-001, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008, REQ-009 (CC-0047)', memcached → 'SC-CHAOS-002, REQ-005, REQ-008, REQ-009 (CC-0047)', openbao → 'SC-CHAOS-003, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-009 (CC-0047)'

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a DECISION comment in chainsaw-config.yaml noting why delete/error/exec timeouts remain at 30s (matching happy-path) instead of the 60s specified in the plan

The Memcached test Step 4 sleep of 30s is a pragmatic choice documented in the reference docs; consider parameterizing this as an env var for CI tuning if reconciliation cycle times vary across environments

Future scenarios could extract the shared catch block diagnostic scripts into a helper ConfigMap or script step to reduce duplication across test files, though the current self-contained approach is acceptable for 3 scenarios
next_steps: Apply the 4 minor fixes (Makefile comment, config timeout comment, requirement IDs in test headers) to complete plan compliance
Proceed to the next layer (L003) once minor feedback is addressed

detected_patterns.name: Chaos E2E test suite structure with degradation/recovery and no-regression patterns
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new Chaos Mesh-based E2E test suite for pod-kill, network partition, or other fault injection scenarios
detected_patterns.description: Each chaos E2E test follows a 6-step structure: (1) Apply Keystone CR, (2) Assert baseline Ready=True (pre-chaos health gate), (3) Apply PodChaos/NetworkChaos CR, (4) Assert expected degradation (sub-condition=False) OR no-regression (all conditions=True after sleep), (5) Delete Chaos CR (explicit fault removal), (6) Assert full recovery (sub-condition=True, Ready=True/AllReady). Two test patterns exist: 'degradation and recovery' for critical dependencies (MariaDB, OpenBao) where a sub-condition must transition to False then back to True, and 'no-regression' for non-critical dependencies (Memcached) where all conditions must remain True during the outage with a 30s sleep before assertion to ensure at least one reconciliation cycle. Each test has a unique CR name (keystone-chaos-{suffix}) and database name (keystone_chaos_{suffix}). Catch blocks on assert steps collect target pod status, Chaos Mesh experiment status, operator/pod logs with --previous, and namespace events.

examples.code:
# ── Step 4: Assert DatabaseReady=False after pod kill ──────────────────
  - try:
    - assert:
        resource:
          apiVersion: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1
          kind: Keystone
          metadata:
            name: keystone-chaos-db
            namespace: openstack
          status:
            (conditions[?type == 'DatabaseReady']):
            - status: "False"

# ── Step 4: Wait for reconciliation and assert Ready=True maintained ───
  - try:
    - sleep:
        duration: 30s
    - assert:
        resource:
          apiVersion: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1
          kind: Keystone
          metadata:
            name: keystone-chaos-mc
            namespace: openstack
          status:
            (conditions[?type == 'SecretsReady']):
            - status: "True"
            (conditions[?type == 'FernetKeysReady']):
            - status: "True"
            (conditions[?type == 'DatabaseReady']):
            - status: "True"
            (conditions[?type == 'DeploymentReady']):
            - status: "True"
            (conditions[?type == 'BootstrapReady']):
            - status: "True"
            (conditions[?type == 'Ready']):
            - status: "True"
              reason: AllReady
